LHX.sentence: L3Harris ticked up a little today, but it's still well below its high of the past year — the business is fine, defense spending is steady, profits are just a touch squeezed right now.

ASML.sentence: ASML dropped almost 5% today for no real reason anyone can point to, which is just normal jitters in an otherwise strong run for the only company that makes these chip-printing machines.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet day — they supply industrial gases like oxygen and nitrogen under long contracts and nudge prices up steadily, which is the whole, undramatic story.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is down nearly a third from its high, which is unusually cheap for such a steady money-maker, and the cloud-and-AI growth story is still very much intact.

TMO.sentence: No fresh read on Thermo Fisher today — nothing to report on the lab-equipment giant.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is quiet, and its dividend payout — while still attractive — isn't as generous a premium over safe bonds as it usually is, so it looks a little richly priced here.

NOW.sentence: No new data came through for ServiceNow today, so nothing to add on the workflow-automation software company.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore slipped a bit, and its mining profits look near a low point in the usual commodity cycle — which for a big miner has historically been when things start looking attractive.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is down again and now sits well below its recent averages, near the low end of its range for the year, so this has been a rough stretch for it.

KOF.sentence: The Mexican Coca-Cola bottler is essentially flat, and the Mexican peso — the main thing that moves it for us — is stable, so it's an uneventful day.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras dipped today even though oil prices are strong, an odd disconnect that's left this Brazilian oil company looking cheap while still paying a hefty dividend.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk fell almost 7% and is now valued below what its fleet of ships would cost to replace — this container-shipping giant tends to move with the ups and downs of global trade.
